rows.title: Arrive at Springdale
rows.planning_note: Arrived before noon, allowing for a relaxed start with one light activity.

rows.title: Pa'rus Trail
rows.notes: Just after you cross the Virgin River on the Canyon Junction Bridge, look to your left for an unobstructed view of the Watchman bathed in evening light.
rows.planning_note: Starting with Pa'rus Trail, the perfect paved, stroller-friendly walk to ease into the Zion experience. Close to the Visitor's Center and accommodating for young families.

rows.title: Lunch at Zion Canyon Brew Pub
rows.notes: Known for its scenic river views. Ideal for families with a children's menu available.
rows.planning_note: Conveniently located near the first day's activities, making midday transition easy.

rows.title: Travel to Zion Human History Museum
Zion Human History Museum
rows.notes: Step to the south-facing patio to capture stunning views of the Towers of the Virgin canyon walls as they glow with the evening light.
rows.planning_note: A perfect midday indoor activity that acclimates the family to Zion's historical context without overwhelming young children.

rows.title: Dinner at Spotted Dog Cafe
rows.notes: Offers a family-friendly menu with diverse options in a casual setting.
rows.planning_note: Ending the day with dinner here to enjoy a comfortable and relaxing atmosphere after a day of exploration.

rows.title: Zion Canyon Shuttle to Temple of Sinawava
rows.notes: Board at Zion Visitor Center stop. Free, runs every 6–8 minutes in peak season. No private vehicles beyond this point.
rows.planning_note: Starting the day early with a shuttle ride to minimize crowd exposure and take advantage of cooler morning temperatures.

rows.title: Riverside Walk
rows.notes: Just past the point where the Riverside Walk curves near the third bend, pause near the rock overhang to your left to catch the enchanting play of sunlight.
rows.planning_note: A refreshing and gentle start ideal for families with young children, offering safe stroller access and beautiful natural views.

rows.title: Picnic Lunch by the Virgin River
rows.notes: Pack a picnic before heading out for the day to enjoy amid the natural beauty of Zion.
rows.planning_note: A relaxed lunch spot along the river, allowing the family to reenergize while soaking in the scenery.

rows.title: Emerald Pools Trail
rows.notes: Pause at the trail junction just past the Lower Emerald Pool to spot the hanging gardens.
rows.planning_note: Scheduled after a relaxing lunch to take advantage of afternoon light. Flexibility allows the family to rest if needed and skip if tired.

rows.title: Dinner at Oscar's Cafe
rows.notes: Known for hearty southwestern fare and an inviting atmosphere—kids are welcome.
rows.planning_note: Dinner here reflects a family-friendly approach, conveniently located for an early dinner after a fulfilling park day.

rows.title: Drive to Kolob Canyons Visitor Center
rows.planning_note: A scenic drive to start your exploration of the less visited parts of Zion, offers an opportunity to encounter vibrant canyon vistas without the crowds.

rows.title: Timber Creek Overlook Trail
rows.notes: Look southward after rain when clouds disperse for a stunning view of Zion's red rock peaks against softened skies.
rows.planning_note: An ideal short, easy trail for families, especially with younger members geared for scenic mornings and unhurried paces.

rows.title: Lunch at Cactus Room Restaurant
rows.notes: Serving fresh and hearty meals within close proximity to Kolob Canyons.
rows.planning_note: Refuel after exploring Kolob, offering a family-friendly menu with quick service.

rows.title: Checkerboard Mesa
rows.notes: Take the short trail on the southern side of the road to the viewpoint hidden just behind the grove of ponderosa pines.
rows.planning_note: A brief, contrast-providing stop helps maintain engagement levels while wrapping up the day’s itinerary with minimal walking required.

rows.title: Dinner at King's Landing Bistro
rows.notes: A popular spot offering refined comfort food, perfectly rounded off after a light day of exploration.
rows.planning_note: Conclude the day with a delightful family meal in a sophisticated yet welcoming setting.

rows.title: Zion Canyon Shuttle to Court of the Patriarchs
rows.notes: Board at Zion Visitor Center stop. Free, runs every 6–8 minutes in peak season.
rows.planning_note: Starting the day early to enjoy autumn foliage while colors are vibrant in the morning light.

rows.title: Autumn Foliage Walk
rows.notes: For a stunning view of autumn foliage contrasted against the towering red cliffs, follow the trail to the base of the Court of the Patriarchs.
rows.planning_note: This walk captures Zion’s beautiful fall colors, timed during optimal lighting and cooler temperatures for young families.

rows.title: Lunch at Sol Foods Supermarket
rows.notes: Grab ready-to-go meals and fresh snacks with picnic areas nearby.
rows.planning_note: Ideal spot to stock up on supplies and have a leisurely meal with options catering to all family members.

rows.title: Explore Rockville Ghost Town
rows.notes: Head to the stone ruins at the eastern edge in late afternoon when the setting sun casts long shadows.
rows.planning_note: Post-lunch visit to an easy-to-navigate site, offers families a light yet intriguing look back into local history.

rows.title: Dinner at Bit & Spur Restaurant and Saloon
rows.notes: Eclectic menu with family-friendly ambiance, perfect to end day of exploration.
rows.planning_note: Chosen for its relaxed vibe and accommodating service for families, resolving the day's activities on a pleasant note.

rows.title: Breakfast at Wildcat Willies Ranch Grill & Saloon
rows.notes: Known for its breakfast burritos and country-style breakfast—ideal for a hearty start before travel.
rows.planning_note: Begin departure smoothly with a fulfilling breakfast, allowing time for packing and checkout.

rows.title: Return to Transport Hub
rows.planning_note: Departure day involves direct travel; no activities scheduled to ensure a relaxed exit from Springdale.

change_log: Day 1: Removed evening activities; rearranged schedule to allow only indoor museum and light trail walk for young kids.
Day 2: Removed third activity to comply with family_young schedule cap.
Added planning notes regarding flexibility for all afternoon activities, important for family_young parties.
Day 3: Added rest slot after drive from Kolob to Springdale for recharging.
Day 4: Adjusted travel and activity planning to ensure family_young compatibility with free afternoon.
Adjusted activities and meals on restricted days to ensure compliance with family_young party type.
